Class EventController

java.lang.Object
planner.controller.EventController
public class EventController extends Object
EventController manages all event-related operations including creation, updating, deletion, and retrieval of events. This controller acts as the business logic layer between the View and the data storage layer.

Architecture Pattern - MVC Controller: This class implements the Controller component of the Model-View-Controller pattern, mediating between the MainFrame (View) and the planner.model classes (Model), while delegating persistence to DataStorage.

Responsibilities:

  • Regular single events and recurring events management
  • Event validation and persistence
  • Attendee management and assignment
  • Event search and filtering capabilities
  • Event export functionality to file formats
  • Data integrity through validation and error handling

Event Management: The controller maintains an in-memory list of events for fast access during runtime and uses DataStorage to persist changes to disk. All modifications go through the controller to ensure data consistency.

Version:
2.0
Author:
Gustavo Henrique Nogueira de Andrade Filho, Pedro Rocha Dantas, Leonardo Oliveira Eid
See Also:
  • DataStorage
  • Event
  • RecurringEvent
  • InvalidEventException

  • Constructor Details

    • EventController

      public EventController()
      Constructs an EventController with default storage configuration.

      Initializes the controller by creating a default DataStorage instance and loading all events from the standard "events_data.txt" file. This is the primary constructor used for production code.

    • EventController

      public EventController(DataStorage storage)
      Constructs an EventController with a custom storage instance.

      Initializes the controller with the provided DataStorage instance and loads all events from storage into memory. This constructor supports Dependency Injection, allowing for flexible storage backends and testable code.

      Legacy Migration: If legacy recurring events without series identifiers are detected, new identifiers are automatically assigned and the updated events are saved back to storage.

      Parameters:
      storage - The DataStorage instance to use for persistence operations

  • Method Details

    • createEvent

      public Event createEvent(String title, LocalDateTime dateTime, String location, String description, String category, int reminderMinutes) throws InvalidEventException
      Creates a new single (non-recurring) event and persists it to storage.

      Execution Steps:

      1. Validates all required input fields (title, dateTime, category)
      2. Creates a new Event object with the provided information
      3. Adds the event to the in-memory event list
      4. Persists the updated event list to file storage
      5. Returns the created event for further use by the caller
      Parameters:
      title - The title or name of the event. Must be non-empty and non-null
      dateTime - The date and time when the event occurs. Must be non-null
      location - The physical location or venue of the event. May be null or empty
      description - Additional details, notes, or agenda items about the event. May be null or empty
      category - The category or type of the event (e.g., "Meeting", "Birthday"). Must be non-empty and non-null
      reminderMinutes - Number of minutes before the event to trigger a reminder notification
      Returns:
      The created Event object containing all provided information
      Throws:
      InvalidEventException - If any required field is null, empty, or contains only whitespace

    • createRecurringEvent

      public RecurringEvent createRecurringEvent(String title, LocalDateTime dateTime, String location, String description, String category, int reminderMinutes, RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType recurrenceType, int repeat) throws InvalidEventException
      Creates a new recurring event with specified recurrence pattern and persists all occurrences to storage.

      Execution Logic: This method generates multiple instances of the same event based on the recurrence type and repeat count. For example, if you specify RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType.DAILY with repeat=5, it will create 5 event instances, each 1 day apart, all sharing the same series identifier.

      Execution Steps:

      1. Validates all required input fields
      2. Validates that recurrence type is not RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType.NONE
      3. Generates a unique series identifier using UUID.randomUUID()
      4. Creates the initial RecurringEvent instance
      5. Generates (repeat - 1) future occurrences based on the recurrence pattern
      6. Persists all event instances to storage as a single atomic operation
      Parameters:
      title - The title or name of the recurring event. Must be non-empty and non-null
      dateTime - The date and time of the first occurrence. Must be non-null
      location - The physical location or venue. May be null or empty
      description - Additional event details. May be null or empty
      category - The event category. Must be non-empty and non-null
      reminderMinutes - Number of minutes before each occurrence to trigger a reminder
      recurrenceType - The pattern for recurrence:
      • RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType.DAILY: Repeats every day
      • RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType.WEEKLY: Repeats every week
      • RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType.MONTHLY: Repeats every month
      • RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType.NONE: Not allowed, throws exception
      repeat - The number of times this event should repeat (total occurrences). For example, repeat=3 creates 3 events spaced according to the recurrence type
      Returns:
      The initial RecurringEvent object that was created (the first occurrence)
      Throws:
      InvalidEventException - If any field is invalid, empty, recurrence type is RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType.NONE, or repeat is not valid
      See Also:
      • RecurringEvent.RecurrenceType

    • updateEvent

      public void updateEvent(Event event, String title, LocalDateTime dateTime, String location, String description, String category, int reminderMinutes) throws InvalidEventException
      Updates an existing single event with new information.

      This method modifies all fields of the given event object in-place (since it's already part of the in-memory event list) and immediately persists the changes to storage.

      Parameters:
      event - The Event object to update. Must already exist in the events list
      title - The new title for the event. Must be non-empty and non-null
      dateTime - The new date and time. Must be non-null
      location - The new location. May be null or empty
      description - The new description. May be null or empty
      category - The new category. Must be non-empty and non-null
      reminderMinutes - The new reminder time in minutes before the event
      Throws:
      InvalidEventException - If any required field is null, empty, or contains only whitespace

    • updateFutureOccurrences

      public void updateFutureOccurrences(RecurringEvent event, String title, LocalDateTime dateTime, String location, String description, String category, int reminderMinutes) throws InvalidEventException
      Updates all future occurrences of a recurring event starting from a specified instance.

      Behavior: This method finds all occurrences of the recurring event with the same series ID that happen at or after (using date/time comparison) the given event instance and applies the updates to all of them. This enables bulk editing of a recurring event series while preserving past occurrences.

      Time Shift Calculation: To maintain the recurring pattern when modifying dates, the method calculates the time difference between the original event's date/time and the new date/time, then applies this same shift to all future occurrences. For example, if the specified occurrence is moved forward by 1 hour, all subsequent occurrences are also shifted forward by 1 hour.

      Parameters:
      event - The RecurringEvent instance to start updating from. This event and all later occurrences in the same series will be updated
      title - The new title for all future occurrences. Must be non-empty and non-null
      dateTime - The new date and time for this specific occurrence. The time shift will be applied to all future occurrences. Must be non-null
      location - The new location for all future occurrences. May be null or empty
      description - The new description for all future occurrences. May be null or empty
      category - The new category for all future occurrences. Must be non-empty and non-null
      reminderMinutes - The new reminder time for all future occurrences
      Throws:
      InvalidEventException - If any required field is null, empty, or contains only whitespace

    • deleteEvent

      public void deleteEvent(Event event)
      Deletes a single event from the system.

      Removes the event from the in-memory list and persists the updated list to storage. If the event is a recurring event, only that specific occurrence is deleted. To delete all future occurrences of a recurring event, use deleteFutureOccurrences(RecurringEvent).

      Parameters:
      event - The Event object to delete. Must exist in the events list

    • deleteFutureOccurrences

      public void deleteFutureOccurrences(RecurringEvent event)
      Deletes all future occurrences of a recurring event starting from a specified instance.

      This method removes the specified occurrence and all subsequent occurrences of the same recurring event (identified by matching series ID), while preserving past occurrences that happen before the specified event.

      Example: If a daily event is scheduled for 10 consecutive days and you call this method on day 5, occurrences 5-10 are deleted while occurrences 1-4 are preserved.

      Parameters:
      event - The RecurringEvent instance to start deleting from. This event and all later occurrences in the same series will be removed

    • getEventsForDate

      public List<Event> getEventsForDate(LocalDate date)
      Retrieves all events scheduled for a specific date.

      Filters all events by the given date (ignoring time of day) and returns them sorted chronologically by time of day. This is commonly used by the calendar view to display all events for a selected date.

      Parameters:
      date - The LocalDate to retrieve events for
      Returns:
      A List of Event objects scheduled for the given date, sorted by time of day in ascending order. Returns an empty list if no events are scheduled for that date

    • getDatesWithEventsInMonth

      public List<LocalDate> getDatesWithEventsInMonth(int year, int month)
      Retrieves all distinct dates in a specific month that have at least one event.

      Returns a list of unique LocalDate objects representing each day in the specified month that has one or more scheduled events. This is particularly useful for calendar UI components that need to highlight or mark days with events.

      Parameters:
      year - The year (e.g., 2024, 2025)
      month - The month as an integer (1-12, where 1 is January and 12 is December)
      Returns:
      A List of distinct LocalDate objects for each day in the specified month that has at least one event. Returns an empty list if no events exist in that month

    • searchEvents

      public List<Event> searchEvents(String keyword)
      Searches for events matching a keyword in multiple fields.

      Search Scope: The search is case-insensitive and looks for substring matches in the following event fields:

      • Event title
      • Event location
      • Event description
      • Event category

      An event matches if the keyword appears in any of these fields, allowing flexible searches across multiple aspects of events.

      Parameters:
      keyword - The search term to look for. The search is case-insensitive and finds substring matches
      Returns:
      A List of Event objects that match the search criteria. Returns an empty list if no events match the keyword or if the keyword is null/empty

    • addAttendee

      public void addAttendee(Event event, String name, String email) throws InvalidEventException
      Adds a single attendee to an event.

      Validates the attendee information (name and email) and adds a new Attendee object to the event's attendee list. The changes are immediately persisted to storage.

      Parameters:
      event - The Event object to add the attendee to
      name - The full name of the attendee. Must be non-empty after trimming whitespace
      email - The email address of the attendee. Must contain the "@" symbol for basic validation
      Throws:
      InvalidEventException - If the name is empty or the email is invalid (missing "@" symbol)

    • addAttendeeToFutureOccurrences

      public void addAttendeeToFutureOccurrences(RecurringEvent event, String name, String email) throws InvalidEventException
      Adds a single attendee to all future occurrences of a recurring event.

      Finds all occurrences of the recurring event with the same series ID that happen at or after the specified event instance and adds the same attendee to each of them. This enables bulk attendee assignment for a recurring event series.

      Parameters:
      event - The RecurringEvent instance to start adding attendees from. This event and all later occurrences in the same series will have the attendee added
      name - The full name of the attendee. Must be non-empty after trimming whitespace
      email - The email address of the attendee. Must contain the "@" symbol for basic validation
      Throws:
      InvalidEventException - If the name is empty or the email is invalid (missing "@" symbol)

    • exportDayToFile

      public void exportDayToFile(LocalDate date, String filePath) throws IOException
      Exports all events for a specific day to a formatted text file.

      Creates a new file at the specified path containing a formatted list of all events scheduled for the given date. Each event's title, formatted date/time, location, and category are written to the file. If no events exist for the day, the file will indicate that no events are scheduled.

      File Format: The exported file includes:

      • Header with the date
      • Formatted event entries with title, time, location, and category
      • Separator lines for readability
      • Message if no events are scheduled
      Parameters:
      date - The LocalDate to export events for
      filePath - The file path where the export should be saved. Examples:
      • Unix/Linux: "/tmp/events_2024-01-15.txt"
      • Windows: "C:\\exports\\events.txt"
      • Relative: "./exports/events_today.txt"
      Throws:
      IOException - If there is an error writing to the file, such as:
      • Invalid file path
      • Insufficient permissions to write to the directory
      • Disk space issues

    • getAllEvents

      public List<Event> getAllEvents()
      Retrieves a copy of all events currently stored in the system.

      Returns a new ArrayList containing all Event objects in the controller. By returning a copy rather than a direct reference to the internal list, this method prevents external code from directly modifying the controller's internal state. All modifications should go through the controller's dedicated methods like updateEvent(Event, String, LocalDateTime, String, String, String, int), deleteEvent(Event), and addAttendee(Event, String, String).

      Returns:
      A new ArrayList containing copies of all Event objects in the system. Returns an empty list if no events exist. Modifications to the returned list do not affect the controller's internal state
